2603.29977.title: Quantifying Cross-Modal Interactions in Multimodal Glioma Survival Prediction via InterSHAP: Evidence for Additive Signal Integration
2603.29977.paper_summary: Multimodal deep learning for cancer prognosis is commonly assumed to benefit from synergistic cross-modal interactions, yet this assumption has not been directly tested in survival prediction settings. This work adapts InterSHAP, a Shapley interaction index-based metric, from classification to Cox proportional hazards models and applies it to quantify cross-modal interactions in glioma survival prediction. Using TCGA-GBM and TCGA-LGG data (n=575), we evaluate four fusion architectures combining whole-slide image (WSI) and RNA-seq features. Our central finding is an inverse relationship between predictive performance and measured interaction: architectures achieving superior discrimination (C-index 0.64$\to$0.82) exhibit equivalent or lower cross-modal interaction (4.8\%$\to$3.0\%). Variance decomposition reveals stable additive contributions across all architectures (WSI${\approx}$40\%, RNA${\approx}$55\%, Interaction${\approx}$4\%), indicating that performance gains arise from complementary signal aggregation rather than learned synergy. These findings provide a practical model auditing tool for comparing fusion strategies, reframe the role of architectural complexity in multimodal fusion, and have implications for privacy-preserving federated deployment.
2603.29977.paper_summary_zh: 多模態深度學習在癌症預後中通常被認為受益於協同的跨模態互動，然而這一假設在生存預測環境中尚未得到直接測試。這項工作將基於Shapley互動指數的InterSHAP從分類適應到Cox比例風險模型，並應用於量化膠質瘤生存預測中的跨模態互動。使用TCGA-GBM和TCGA-LGG數據（n=575），我們評估了四種融合架構，結合了全片影像（WSI）和RNA-seq特徵。我們的主要發現是預測性能與測量的互動之間存在反比關係：實現優越區分（C指數0.64$\to$0.82）的架構顯示出相等或更低的跨模態互動（4.8\%$\to$3.0\%）。方差分解顯示所有架構之間的穩定加性貢獻（WSI${\approx}$40\%，RNA${\approx}$55\%，互動${\approx}$4\%），表明性能提升來自於互補信號的聚合，而非學習的協同。這些發現為比較融合策略提供了一個實用的模型審計工具，重新框架了架構複雜性在多模態融合中的角色，並對隱私保護的聯邦部署具有啟示意義。
2603.29977.author: Iain Swift et.al.
2603.29977.authors: Iain Swift, JingHua Ye, Ruairi O'Reilly
